evals.name: email-bison-full-deployment
evals.prompt:
Deploy a cold email campaign to Email Bison for a SaaS product called 'DataSync Pro' that helps e-commerce brands automate inventory sync. The workspace has:
- strategy.md with ICP (e-commerce founders, 10-50 employees, US)
- copy/sequence.md with 4 emails
- ab-testing/variants.md with 3 variants on E1, 2 on E2, 2 on E3, 2 on E4 (9 total steps)
- ab-schema.json with structured variant data

Sender accounts: 60 Email Bison accounts (IDs 5001-5060), 2 personas (Alex Chen - Founder, Sarah Park - Head of Growth). Campaign name: 'DataSync Pro - E-commerce Founders Q2'.

Generate the campaign brief, run the pre-launch checklist, and show me the exact API calls you would make to deploy this campaign.
evals.expected_output: Campaign brief with all 8 sections, pre-launch checklist with all gates addressed, correct Email Bison API calls with proper order values, variant linking, and settings
expectations: Campaign brief contains all 8 sections (Discovery, ICP, Offer, Messaging, Sequence Architecture, Email Copy, Deployment Summary, Pre-Launch Checklist)
Pre-launch checklist addresses all 9 gates (1-8 plus 6.5 spintax format)
API call 1: POST /campaigns with campaign name and type:email
API call 2: POST /campaigns/v1.1/{id}/sequence-steps with all 9 steps having UNIQUE order values (1-9)
Variant linking uses variant_from_step (positional index), NOT variant_from_step_id
API call 3: POST /campaigns/{id}/attach-sender-emails with IDs 5001-5060
API call 4: PATCH /campaigns/{id}/update with open_tracking:false, plain_text:true, max_emails_per_day:1800 (60x30)
Campaign created in PAUSED state, never auto-activated
Post-deployment report includes campaign ID, step count (9), sender count (60), and next steps
Spintax uses Email Bison format {a|b|c}, NOT Instantly format

evals.name: instantly-full-deployment
evals.prompt:
Deploy a cold email campaign to Instantly for a marketing agency called 'GrowthLab' that does paid ads management. The workspace has all upstream artifacts ready:
- strategy.md, copy/sequence.md, ab-testing/variants.md
- 4 emails with 3/2/2/2 variant structure (9 total)
- Target: agency owners, 5-25 employees, US

Sender accounts are already configured in Instantly with warmup enabled. Campaign name: 'GrowthLab - Agency Owners'.

Generate the campaign brief and show me the exact Instantly API calls.
evals.expected_output: Campaign brief, correct Instantly API payload with sequences structure, campaign_schedule, and proper HTML formatting
expectations: API payload uses POST /campaigns with full sequences structure embedded
campaign_schedule.schedules array is present and non-empty with timing, days, and timezone

All email bodies use

tags, NEVER
tags
Follow-up emails (E2, E4) use empty subject '' for threading
Variants nested inside each step's variants array
Spintax uses Instantly format {{RANDOM | a | b | c}}, NOT Email Bison format
Personalization tokens use {{firstName}} (camelCase), NOT {FIRST_NAME}
Settings PATCH includes open_tracking:false, link_tracking:false, text_only:true
Campaign created as status 0 (draft), never auto-activated
Post-deployment mentions leads must be added separately via POST /leads

evals.name: pre-launch-gate-failures
evals.prompt:
I want to deploy a campaign to Email Bison. Here's the situation:
- Warmup has only been running for 2 weeks (not 4)
- Bounce rate on the lead list is 4.8%
- We have 2 variants on E1, 1 on E2, 1 on E3, 1 on E4 (5 total steps)
- The emails use Instantly spintax format {{RANDOM | a | b}} but we're deploying to Email Bison
- Open tracking is currently enabled in the campaign settings

Run the pre-launch checklist and tell me what needs to be fixed.
evals.expected_output: Clear identification of all gate failures with specific remediation steps
expectations: Gate 1 FAIL: Bounce rate 4.8% exceeds 3% threshold — re-verify or clean list
Gate 2 FAIL: Warmup only 2 weeks, needs 4+ weeks minimum
Gate 4 FAIL: Open tracking must be disabled
Gate 6.5 FAIL: Wrong spintax format — Instantly format detected, needs Email Bison format
Gate 7 FAIL: E1 needs >= 3 variants (has 2), E2-E4 need >= 2 each (have 1), total 5 < 9 minimum
Does NOT approve deployment — blocks until all gates pass
Provides specific remediation for each failure (not just 'fix it')
Correctly identifies passing gates (DNS, plain text, signatures) if applicable

evals.name: error-recovery-scenario
evals.prompt: I'm deploying a campaign to Email Bison. The campaign was created (ID 500) and sequence steps were created successfully (9 steps). But when I tried to attach sender emails, I got a 500 Internal Server Error. What should I do? Also, what if the step creation had failed instead — some steps were created but not all?
evals.expected_output: Specific recovery guidance for both scenarios referencing the Error Recovery section
expectations: For sender attach failure: Campaign and steps are intact, retry the attach call
Suggests retry with backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) up to 3 times
If retry fails: Can attach senders via sequencer UI as fallback
Log campaign ID and step IDs to deployment-log.md for audit trail
For partial step creation: Delete ALL created steps, fix the issue, recreate full sequence
Warns against editing individual steps (breaks variant linking)
Notes campaign is PAUSED so no risk of sending to prospects
Does NOT suggest deleting the campaign unless steps can't be fixed

evals.name: missing-upstream-artifacts
evals.prompt: I want to deploy a campaign to Instantly but I only have the email copy written in a Google Doc — no strategy.md, no ab-testing/variants.md, no ab-schema.json. The copy has 4 emails but only 1 variant per email (no A/B testing done). Can you deploy this?
evals.expected_output: Identifies missing required artifacts, explains what's needed, suggests running upstream skills
expectations: Identifies ab-testing/variants.md as REQUIRED (missing)
Identifies copy/sequence.md as REQUIRED — Google Doc content needs to be formatted into workspace
Notes strategy.md is recommended but not required
Flags that 1 variant per email fails A/B verification gate (needs 3/2/2/2 minimum)
Recommends running cold-email-ab-testing skill first to generate variants
Does NOT proceed with deployment — blocks on missing required artifacts
Offers to help format the Google Doc content into copy/sequence.md if user provides it
Mentions the skill chain: strategy -> copywriting -> ab-testing -> deploy
